_meta.notes: Vermont-specific cost tiers. Numbers calibrated against named local sources cited per trade. Update quarterly. Labor multipliers handled at the town level (towns-vermont.json), not here.

surface_refresh.label: Surface refresh
surface_refresh.scope_notes: Repaint or refinish existing cabinets, new counters, new appliances, new lighting, no layout changes, no plumbing relocation.

mid_range.label: Mid-range
mid_range.scope_notes: New semi-custom cabinetry, quartz or solid-surface counters, mid-range appliance package, possible plumbing relocation, lighting upgrades. Existing footprint.

high_end.label: Full gut or high-end
high_end.scope_notes: Custom cabinetry, premium appliances, structural changes (walls removed, layout reworked), high-end finishes.

sources.name: Woodstock Kitchens & Baths
sources.notes: Publishes tiered pricing on their site.

what_pushes_high: Moving plumbing or gas lines (each relocation is $2,000-5,000)
Pre-1940 housing stock (older electrical and plumbing surprises)
Hardwood floor refinishing during the reno
Whole-house electrical update if panel is undersized
not_included: Town permit fee (varies; see town-specific data)

cosmetic.label: Cosmetic refresh
cosmetic.scope_notes: New paint, new fixtures, new vanity, no tile work, no plumbing relocation.

standard.label: Standard remodel
standard.scope_notes: New tile floor and shower, new vanity, updated plumbing fixtures, new exhaust fan.

complete.label: Full gut
complete.scope_notes: Move plumbing, rework layout, custom shower or tub, premium finishes.

what_pushes_high: Moving the toilet or shower drain
Hidden water damage discovered during demo
Custom tile work vs. standard subway

large_or_composite.label: Large deck or composite material
large_or_composite.scope_notes: Wraparound, multi-level, or composite (Trex etc.). Composite adds 30-50% over PT.

sources.name: Today's Homeowner Vermont deck cost data

what_pushes_high: Permits and engineered footings on sloped sites

repair_seal.label: Crack fill and seal coat
repair_seal.scope_notes: Existing asphalt is intact; sealing and filling cracks only.

full_replacement.label: Full asphalt replacement
full_replacement.scope_notes: Tear out and replace, including base prep.

what_pushes_high: Steep grade requiring extra base prep

asphalt_basic.label: Asphalt shingle (standard)
asphalt_basic.scope_notes: Architectural asphalt shingles, tear-off of one layer, standard ranch or cape.

asphalt_complex.label: Asphalt shingle (complex roof)
asphalt_complex.scope_notes: Multiple gables, dormers, or steep pitch. Most VT farmhouses fall here.

metal_standing_seam.label: Standing seam metal
metal_standing_seam.scope_notes: Higher upfront, lasts 50+ years, sheds snow well.

what_pushes_high: Ice and water shield to code (Vermont requires more than warmer states)

ductless_single.label: Ductless single-zone
ductless_single.scope_notes: One indoor head, one outdoor unit. Good for one main room.

ducted_central.label: Ducted central heat pump
ducted_central.scope_notes: Replaces or supplements existing forced-air system. Whole-house heating and cooling.

sources.name: Vermont Energy Excellence Network installer quotes

what_pushes_high: Older home requiring electrical panel upgrade ($2,000-3,500)
Removal of existing oil tank or boiler

whole_house_diy.label: Whole-house DIY weatherization kit
whole_house_diy.scope_notes: Weatherstripping, foam outlet gaskets, pipe insulation, basic air-sealing.

targeted.label: Targeted air sealing + attic insulation
targeted.scope_notes: Most common entry point. Attic blow-in insulation, basic air sealing of penetrations.

comprehensive.label: Whole-house weatherization
comprehensive.scope_notes: Attic + basement + air sealing across the building envelope.

what_pushes_high: Older home with knob-and-tube wiring requiring remediation first
Mold or moisture issues discovered (Home Repair Program may help)
Larger home or unusual envelope (multi-story, additions)
not_included: Pre-weatherization repairs (covered by Home Repair Program if income-eligible)
